sources_searched: Pluto Charon Nix Hydra cycler trajectory periodic orbit

Nix Hydra small moons spacecraft tour mission trajectory patched conic
Pluto small satellites Nix Hydra Styx Kerberos flyby tour mission design
Persephone Pluto orbiter trajectory periodic orbits Nix Hydra Charon flyby tour
Pluto Charon CR3BP three-body periodic orbits Lyapunov halo distant retrograde DRO
"Hydra-Nix" OR "Nix-Hydra" cycler resonant transfer orbit Pluto system
"Charon-Nix" cycler ballistic moon tour Pluto trajectory design
Pluto Charon multiple moon flyby trajectory tour resonant repeating orbit
Pluto satellite system Lambert resonant orbit periodic cycler design AAS AIAA
Brozovic Showalter Pluto small moons resonance orbits cycler
three-body resonance Styx Nix Hydra Laplace-like spacecraft tour
Pluto-Charon cycler low-thrust OR ballistic trajectory tour moons site:arxiv.org
Pluto-Charon binary cr3bp mass ratio 0.108 periodic orbit family novel discovery 2024 2025 2026
literature_check.notes: Live WebSearch pass (#269) over 14 structured queries covering each unique tour structure (Hydra-Nix-Hydra, Nix-Charon-Nix, Nix-Hydra-Nix, Charon-Nix-Charon, Charon-Hydra-Nix-Charon, Hydra-Nix-Charon-Hydra, Nix-Charon-Hydra-Nix) plus Pluto-Charon system, CR3BP, named-author (Stern/Brozovic/Showalter/Howard), and arxiv-targeted backstops. The Pluto-Charon spacecraft-trajectory literature divides cleanly into (a) Persephone-class CR3BP periodic orbits in the rotating frame (structurally distinct: orbits AROUND the binary, not patched-conic Lambert legs between moons), (b) Stern/SwRI Charon-gravity-assist orbiter tours (explicitly not periodic cyclers), and (c) natural celestial-mechanics of the moons themselves (Showalter, Brozovic). NO paper describes patched-conic ballistic moon-to-moon Lambert cyclers in the Pluto system (Hydra<->Nix<->Hydra etc.). NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: web search is partial, the Persephone full PDF was not opened, and the structural fingerprint may not survive into text. The human + V0-V5 gauntlet still govern.

nearest_literature.paper: Howard et al., 'Persephone: A Pluto-System Orbiter and Kuiper Belt Explorer'
nearest_literature.what_it_is: spacecraft science orbits in Pluto/Charon CR3BP (high out-of-plane + low-altitude periodic orbits in the rotating frame); incidental encounters with small moons
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: different family: CR3BP periodic orbits around the binary, NOT patched-conic ballistic Lambert legs Hydra->Nix->Hydra etc; no 'cycler' terminology

nearest_literature.paper: Stern, Tapley, Zangari et al., SwRI Pluto-orbiter Charon-gravity-assist tour (2018 DPS / Game-Changer)
nearest_literature.ref: DPS 2018; popular write-ups Space.com / Centauri Dreams
nearest_literature.what_it_is: Charon-anchored gravity-assist tour with dozens of Charon flybys and 5+ flybys of each small moon
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: explicitly NOT a periodic cycler -- one-off sequential gravity assists; article confirms no 'cycler/periodic/resonant/repeating-pattern' terminology

nearest_literature.paper: arXiv:2510.13479 (2025), 'Chaotic Dynamics and Zero-Velocity Structures in the Pluto-Charon CR3BP'
nearest_literature.what_it_is: tadpole/horseshoe orbits around L4/L5/L3 in Pluto-Charon CR3BP (instability validation)
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: Trojan-class librating orbits around triangular Lagrange points, not moon-to-moon ballistic tours

nearest_literature.paper: Showalter & Hamilton 2015, 'Resonant interactions and chaotic rotation of Pluto's small moons'
nearest_literature.what_it_is: natural three-body Laplace-like resonance among Styx-Nix-Hydra (3lambda_S - 5lambda_N + 2lambda_H ~ 180deg); chaotic rotation
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: celestial mechanics of the moons themselves, not spacecraft trajectories

nearest_literature.paper: Brozovic et al. 2015 (orbits/masses); Brozovic 2022 'Orbit determination of the moons of the Pluto-Charon system'
nearest_literature.what_it_is: orbit determination of Pluto's moons (n-body fits to HST observations)
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: moon orbit-determination, not spacecraft cycler design

nearest_literature.paper: Pires da Silva Aljbaae et al. (A&A 2023) -- Orbital analysis of the Pluto-Charon moon system's mutual interactions and forced frequencies
nearest_literature.what_it_is: natural moon dynamics analysis -- forced frequencies, mutual interactions
nearest_literature.reason_no_match: natural moon dynamics, not spacecraft

The Pluto-Charon spacecraft-trajectory literature divides cleanly into (a) Persephone-class CR3BP periodic orbits in the rotating frame (structurally distinct: orbits AROUND the binary, not patched-conic Lambert legs between moons), (b) Stern/SwRI Charon-gravity-assist orbiter tours (explicitly not periodic cyclers), and (c) natural celestial-mechanics of the moons themselves (Showalter, Brozovic). literature_check.discipline: not-found is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT for novelty: absence of a search hit is not evidence of absence; the human + V0-V5 gauntlet still govern. This is a rediscovery filter, not a novelty proof.
